rationale.R1.1: Restructuring section flow requires correctly identifying which paragraph carries the gap statement (para 2: 'None, to our knowledge…'), which carries the contribution claim (para 4: 'The principal contribution…'), and which are results vs. methods — misreading their conceptual roles would produce an incoherent reordering.
rationale.R1.5: This is the entire delegation: reordering four paragraphs whose current sequence (Results → Related Work → Model → Contribution) inverts the standard IMRaD arc; the task is load-bearing because an incorrect reordering directly fails the request. Also requires noting that para 4 contains a forward reference ('results reported in the opening paragraph') that breaks under any reordering and must be flagged or repaired.
